## Purpose
Short, actionable guidance for AI coding agents working in this repo (API automation tests).

## Project Layout (what matters)
- `conftest.py`: defines `--run-api` option and fixtures: `base_url`, `default_headers`, `encrypt_key`.
- `pytest.ini`: test discovery rules and `addopts` (runs `-vs --html=reports/report.html`).
- `common/`: helper modules: `http_utils.py`, `sign_utils.py`, `excel_utils.py` (empty).
- `config/settings.py`: central constants and header builders (`build_common_encrypted_headers`, `build_login_headers`).
- `tests/`: pytest test suites (e.g., `test_login_phone.py`, `test_registered.py`) that demonstrate request patterns and CSV-driven parametrization.
- `data/`: CSV fixtures: `login_phone.csv`, `register_phone.csv`, `device_ids.csv`.

## Quick developer workflows
- Install deps: `pip install -r requirements.txt` from `ApiAutomation` root.
- Run all tests (HTML report auto-generated by `pytest.ini`):
  - `pytest`
- Run only API tests (they are marked `@pytest.mark.api`) and allow real external calls:
  - `pytest -m api --run-api`
- Run a single test file (useful for local debugging):
  - `pytest tests/test_login_phone.py::test_login_phone_api --run-api`

## Important patterns & conventions (do not change without tests)
- Tests and helpers often mutate `sys.path` to import project modules; avoid refactoring to absolute-packages without ensuring imports still work in CI and local runs. See `tests/*` and `common/http_utils.py`.
- `HttpUtils.post(...)` will encrypt payloads when `encrypt_key` is provided; it also adds `locale`, `timestamp`, and `sign` headers. See `common/http_utils.py` and `common/sign_utils.py` for exact behavior.
- `sign_utils.generate_sign(...)` expects filtered JSON, lowercased keys and special handling for keys starting with `stay` (it strips the prefix). Preserve these transformations if you touch signing/encryption logic.
- `config/settings.py` holds the authoritative `BASE_URL`, `TEST_ENCRYPT_KEY`, and helper header builders — update here when changing global defaults.

## Data & parametrization
- Tests load phone numbers and device ids from CSV files in `data/`. Keep CSV column names (`phone_number`, `uniqueId`) consistent with existing readers in `tests/`.
- `tests/test_registered.py` implements a balanced random allocation of phones→uniqueId with `random.seed(42)` for reproducibility; respect the seed if changing randomness.

## Response handling and heuristics
- Tests use tolerant success checks (see `_is_login_success` in `tests/test_login_phone.py`) — they check multiple common keys (`stayCode`, `code`, `token`, `success`, etc.). When modifying assertion behavior prefer to update this helper rather than scatter changes across tests.

## Examples (copyable)
- Call encrypted POST (as tests do):

```python
from common.http_utils import HttpUtils
from config import settings

payload = {...}
headers = settings.build_common_encrypted_headers()
resp = HttpUtils.post(url=f"{settings.BASE_URL}{settings.LOGIN_PHONE_PATH}",
                     data=payload,
                     headers=headers,
                     encrypt_key=settings.TEST_ENCRYPT_KEY)
```

## Notes for AI agents
- Prefer small, targeted edits and run tests locally (`pytest`) after changes.
- Do not remove or silently change `--run-api` gating: CI/local runs rely on it to avoid hitting external services.
- `excel_utils.py` is currently unused/empty — do not assume functionality exists there.
- Use the fixtures in `conftest.py` for tests rather than hardcoding URLs/keys.

If anything here is unclear or you want more examples (e.g., typical request/response shapes or CI setup), tell me which area to expand.
